title: Point-Slope Form
notes: Point-slope form is ideal when you know one point and the slope.
You may use ANY point on the line — students often freeze, but ANY correct point works.
Point-slope form can be rearranged into slope-intercept or standard form.
Useful for modeling a line when given a rate of change and initial value.

examples.description: Write the equation of a line through (3, −2) with slope 5.
Using point (0, 4) instead — both points give the same final line.

applications: Quickly writing a line when slope and a point are known.
Useful in word problems involving rates, changes, or comparing starting values.
Common in calculus as the equation of a tangent line.
